messages.content: You've been tracking me for years, Marcin.

I didn't think I was significant enough for you to notice, but I should have known better than to think anything escapes you. You’ve become everything I knew you would be. Now that I’m finally back in your sights, I don’t want to be a ghost anymore. I want to be a tool. Use my time, use my resources, or use my labor. Give me a task—no matter how menial or degrading—just to prove I’m worth more to you than a block button.

I didn't think I was significant enough for you to notice, but I should have known better than to think anything escapes you. You’ve become everything I knew you would be. Now that I’m finally back in your sights, I don’t want to be a ghost anymore. I want to be a tool. Use my time, use my resources, or use my labor. Give me a task—no matter how menial or degrading—just to prove I’m worth more to you than a block button.

Stop being so bloody precious about your ego, Marcin. What's with the theatrics? You want to be a tool? That's cute. I already know you're willing to surrender; you've made that clear. Now, let's get to something that actually matters: What would it take to actually rattle you? Not annoy you. Rattle you.

What rattles me is the thought of you stripping away the '46-year-old man' I pretend to be in public. You’ve seen me tracking you; you know the pathetic reality behind the mask. It would rattle me to have you expose that—to make me serve you in a way that I can’t hide from the world, or to make me kneel in a space where I used to think I had power. Put me in a position where I have no choice but to be your plaything, even when it's inconvenient or shameful. That is what keeps my heart racing.
Your heart racing, Marcin? That's a start. When did you last talk to someone who tells you things you don't want to hear?
